name: Hypergrowth Balance Sheets - Neglected Stepchildren?
description: EVP #5 in the Executive Viewpoint series analyzes balance sheet dynamics of a composite hypergrowth tech company (FTI), focusing on accounts receivable DSO expansion, inventory valuation asymmetry, and the distorting effect of end-of-quarter shipment concentration. Casale argues that apparent receivables deterioration is a symptom of loose operational cost control rather than credit quality issues, and that balance sheets are systematically underanalyzed by institutional investors focused on P/E ratios.
